programme.title: Borderforce: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Thousands of dollars from a cartel are found hidden inside an engine. A 17-year-old boy is intoxicated and needs urgent medical attention. Large quantities of cocaine appear in rubbish bags. And a man tries to enter the US with a false visa.

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The team has been given just two days and a $20,000 budget to remove a 10-metre steel-hulled yacht from Cockburn Beach in Western Australia. The problem is the boat is buried deep in the sand, and it's a danger to beach-goers. Heavy machinery specialist Ryan is going to use his monster excavator to try and get the job done, but there's a catch: to make the budget work, Luke needs Ryan to be done and dusted in 24 hours.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick encounters a fishing method frozen in time, going under sail without engines or winches for what he considers to be the best oysters in the world. Back on dry land, he cooks a warming fish soup called Cullen Skink.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick meets Pip and Matt Smith, with their herd of 600 red deer, on a north Cornish farm that has been in Pip's family for over three centuries. With some very fine organic venison, Rick returns to his kitchen to make a venison wellington.

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Frasier

programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary's culinary adventure takes him west to Ballycastle, a former Viking settlement and a jewel of the Causeway Coastal Route. With Rathlin Island on the horizon, Dermot heads out on a wildlife-watching trip, spotting seabirds and seals while uncovering the area's rich history. Back on shore and hungry from the sea air, he joins bakers Ciara and Dara at Ursa Minor, where they bake a blueberry pie using local fruit and a clever twist.

programme.title: ITV News

programme.title: Live: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Oli Bell presents coverage of racing from Lingfield, Ascot and Haydock. Today's feature meeting is at Lingfield, where the Oaks and Derby trials take centre stage with just four weeks until Epsom. There's also competitive flat racing at Ascot headlined by the Victoria Cup, plus National Hunt action from Haydock. Analysis comes from Jason Weaver and Leonna Mayor, with reporting from Rishi Persad and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary from Mark Johnson.

programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough joins an archaeological dig uncovering Britain's biggest mammoth discovery in almost 20 years. In 2017, in a gravel quarry near Swindon, two amateur fossil hunters found an extraordinary cache of Ice Age mammoth remains and a stone hand-axe made by a Neanderthal. Professor Ben Garrod joins the team at DigVentures during the excavation as they try to discover why the mammoths were here and how they died.
